english: tally: disposition, temper, state of mind Thgy.; *ḳo nám-pa-la* (kho rnam pa la) = sám-pa-la (bsam pa la) C. in his mind.

1. termin. of rnám-pa (rnam pa): into the form etc., v. above. — 2. as postp. like, = the Lat. instar, Wdn. — 3. adv. (possibly an abbreviation of rnám-pa (rnam pa) kún-tu (kun tu)), entirely, perfectly, thoroughly; in negative sentences: by no means, on no account; often only adding force to another word, Ssk. वि; frq. in the shorter form rnam (rnam). The following expressions most in use, containing the adv. rnám-par (rnam par) or rnam (rnam), are alphabetically arranged with reference to the second word: rnám-par (rnam par) klúb-pa (klub pa) to adorn, embellish Cs. — rnam-gráṅs (rnam grangs) 1. enumeration, rgyál-poi (rgyal po'i) of kings Glr. 2. the whole amount, sum total, S.g.; full number or quantity, where nothing is wanting Glr. 90, 3.; mtsán-gyi (mtshan gyi) rnam-gráṅs (rnam grangs) the component parts of his name according to their etymological value Tar. 69, 3. 3. treatise, dissertation, a paper, čós-kyi (chos kyi) frq. 4. by grammarians the signification of de (de) is thus defined: rnam-graṅs-gžan-brjód-pa (rnam grangs gzhan brjod pa) demonstrative pronoun(?). — rnam-gyúr (rnam gyur) (cf. above rnám-pa (rnam pa) 5) 1. form, figure, shape, yi-gei (yi ge'i) rnam-gyúr (rnam gyur) the form of the letters (written or printed) Glr., or in this passage also = the graceful form of letters, caligraphy, penmanship, v. below. 2. behaviour, demeanour, lus-ṅág-gi (lus ngag gi) Wdn.; of a sick person S.g.; gesture, e.g. devout gestures Mil.; rnam-ogyúr (rnam 'gyur) rdzés-pa (rdzes pa) Pth. mimic gestures, mimical performance, ballet. More esp.: 3. beautiful form, graceful carriage of the body, graceful attitudes (of dancers etc.) Pth.; bzoi (bzo'i) rnam-gyúr (rnam gyur) the beauty of a work Glr. 4. pride C., W., Mil.; rnám-ogyur-čan (rnam 'gyur can) fine, smart, gayly dressed; proud, vain, foppish col. — rnám-par (rnam par) rgyál-ba (rgyal ba) conquering completely, gaining a full victory Pth.; rnam-rgyál (rnam rgyal) a surname much in use; rnam-rgyal-pún-pa (rnam rgyal pun pa), acc. to Schl. 247 búm-pa (bum pa), water-bottle for sacred uses. — rnam(-par)-bčád(-pa) (rnam (-par) -bcad (-pa)) section, paragraph, rnám-par (rnam par) bčád-pa (bcad pa) daṅ-po-o (dang po'o) first paragraph; also mark of punctuation at the end of a paragraph, i.e. double-shad (shad). — rnam-bču-dbaṅ-ldan (rnam bcu dbang ldan) a certain way of writing the Ommanipadmehūm, v. Schl. p. 121; but I should rather explain it in accordance to rnám-pa (rnam pa) 2, as the 'ten powerful things', scil. letters or written characters, else the words would have been: rnám-par (rnam par) dbaṅ-ldán (dbang ldan) bču (bcu). — rnám-par (rnam par) jóg-pa ('jog pa) v. rnam-bžág (rnam bzhag). — rnám-par (rnam par) rtóg-pa (rtog pa) (cf. rtóg-pa (rtog pa) I. 2, and II., 2), gen. sbst. rnam-rtóg (rnam rtog) (विकल्प distinction; doubt, error) 1. discrimination, perception; so perh. S.g.: rnam-rtóg (rnam rtog) ṅan (ngan) bčòm (bcom) the perception of what is disagreeable is weakened; reasoning, mental investigation, opp. to ye-šes (ye shes), the sublime wisdom of the saint. 2. scruple, hesitation, rnam-rtóg (rnam rtog) ma (ma) mdzád-par (mdzad par) čaṅ (chang) odi ('di) gsól (gsol) please drink this beer without any scruple! Pth.; so also in col. language. 3. in philosophy: obscuration, viz. of the clear and direct (nihilistic) knowledge of truth by reasonings in the mind of the individual, error, Was. (305). 4. in pop. language disgust, distaste, rnám-rtog (rnam rtog) skyéd-pa (skyed pa) to feel disgust Glr., zá-ba (za ba) Pth. prob. id. — rnám-(par) (rnam -(par)) tár(-ba) (thar (-ba)). 1. to be entirely released or delivered, and sbst. complete deliverance, rnam-tár (rnam thar) gsum (gsum) Trigl. fol. 12, three ascetic notions (in themselves of little consequence), stoṅ-pa-ñyíd (stong pa nyid), mtsán-pa-med-pa (mtshan pa med pa), and smón-pa-med-pa (smon pa med pa). 2. sbst. rnam-tár (rnam thar) biography, legendary tales about a saint; tale, story, description, in general. — rnam-tós-(kyi) (rnam thos -(kyi)) bu (bu), sras (sras), rnam-sras (rnam sras) = Kuvera, Ssk. वैश्रवण. — rnam-(par) (rnam -(par)) dág(-pa) (dag (-pa)) thoroughly cleansed, frq.; by rnam-(par) (rnam -(par)) dág(-par) (dag (-par)) rtsi-ba (rtsi ba), or mdzád-pa (mdzad pa) I have attempted to express the Scriptural doctrine of δικαιοῦν or justification. — rnam-odúd (rnam 'dud) n. of one of the seven golden hills round Mount Meru Glr. — rnam-odréṅ (rnam 'dren) (cf. odrén-pa ('dren pa) 2) the saviour, Buddha; rnam-log-odréṅ (rnam log 'dren) the reverse. — rnam-par-snaṅ-mdzád (rnam par snang mdzad), वैरोचन, n. of the first of the Dhyani Buddhas. — rnam-(par) (rnam -(par)) oprul(-ba) ('phrul (-ba)) sorcery, magic tricks, byéd-pa (byed pa) Dom. — rnam-pyé (rnam phye), rnam-pyéd (rnam phyed), prob. = rnam-(par) (rnam -(par)) dbye(-ba) (dbye (-ba)) 1. distinction, division, section. 2. rnam-dbyé (rnam dbye) case or cases, of which the Tibetan gram-
